user_message: Help me build partner engagement for Proseware Inc. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 10:24 EST
Subject: URGENT: partner engagement Issue at Proseware Inc

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 11:50 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 12:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Proseware Inc partner engagement

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Proseware Inc
Date: 2024-03-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Chen (Proseware Inc), Priya Sharma (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Chen (Proseware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:07:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:17:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
